angle: PAINTED BRUSH POSTER. Photo cut out and painted over with gouache brush-strokes in cobalt+cream — the drink becomes a painted gesture not a photo. One italic serif line. Gallery-print register.
subject: Guacamole con Chicharrón
prompt:
A social media image for Blue Mezcal — an upscale agave-forward bar AND kitchen that is SOCIAL, BOLD, FUN, and cocktail-forward. Think of Blue Mezcal as THE BOLD SIBLING OF AZTECA — same cultural heart, more adult, more cocktail, more elevated Mexican food. Reference anchors: Casa Azul NYC (daylight editorial polish + nighttime energy), Handshake Speakeasy Mexico City (social insider bar), Licorería Limantour (rowdy-chic), Cosme (Enrique Olvera's upscale but PACKED and buzzing). NOT quiet. NOT a library. NOT Céline-minimal. Fun, social, bartender-confident — people come here for DRINKS + elevated Mexican dishes.

The graphic identity runs with EDITORIAL POLISH (restrained grids, refined serifs, controlled palette) to signal seriousness — but the activations run with CONFIDENT ENERGY (oversized type, bold color blocks, after-hours moodiness when the register calls for it) to signal life. Editorial discipline ↔ party energy. Never drop either side.

BRAND-IDENTITY ANCHOR (NON-NEGOTIABLE): Blue Mezcal = BLUE + CREAM. Cobalt #1E3A8A and bone cream #F3ECD8 are the DOMINANT 60/30 pairing on every post — one must dominate, the other must support. This is the colour signature of the account. Every post should be recognizable as Blue Mezcal in under one second based on palette alone.

COMPLEMENT PALETTE (ACCENTS ONLY — never dominant, each capped at under 10-15% of canvas):
- Marigold #F2A900 — accent word or ornament only
- Agave green #4E6B3A — occasional pull only
- Chile red #D72638 — complement for warm-dish photos only
- Charred black #1C1A17 — footer / small-caps ink only

FORBIDDEN in the playful register: burgundy-dominant grounds, brass-foil-on-black nocturnal treatment (that's a separate stem), turquoise, hot pink, any color outside the locked palette above.

PRIMARY GROUND OPTIONS (pick one, always with 5–10% tonal gradient — never flat):
- Cobalt #1E3A8A → deep midnight-cobalt #14213D gradient field
- Bone cream #F3ECD8 → warm ivory #E8DBB8 gradient field
- OR a two-field split (cobalt + bone) with the photo bridging or sitting in one field

TYPE TREATMENT: refined editorial serif (Canela / GT Sectra / Tiempos Headline) as hero. Playful moves ALLOWED: italic at odd scale, one word larger than the rest, hairline hand-drawn underline, script+serif dual-weight lockup, oversized type that wraps or interacts with the photo (Chomp City move, earned — max 1 in 6 posts). Body/labels in quiet grotesque small-caps (Söhne / Neue Haas / GT America). White/cream type on cobalt, cobalt type on cream. NEVER use all three colors (cobalt + cream + a complement) at equal weight — palette always reads blue-and-cream first.

COHESION TEST (applies to EVERY post): at least TWO of these three must appear, or the post fails review:
  1. A refined serif in italic OR small-caps, at some scale
  2. Cobalt + bone-cream dominant in 60/30 ratio (one register per post, never mixed with the nocturnal brass/burgundy)
  3. A 5-12% tonal gradient on at least one color field (never a pure flat hex fill)

PHOTO-LED COMPLEMENT RULE (mandatory): analyze the dominant hue of the supplied drink/dish photograph first. Pick the ground colour that OPPOSES that dominant hue:
  - Warm drink (amber, yellow, orange, peach) → cobalt #1E3A8A ground (cool opposes warm)
  - Cool/green drink → bone cream #F3ECD8 or warm marigold accent
  - Dark drink → bone cream ground for pop
  - Light/pale drink → cobalt ground for contrast
State which hex you chose and why before composing.

COMPOSITION ENERGY: asymmetric layouts OK. A single oversized word (the drink/dish name) taking 40–50% of canvas, with a small smart-mouthed supporting phrase nearby. Or a full-bleed photo with a cobalt band wrapping one edge and a marigold hand-drawn accent. Rule-of-thirds, tension, movement. NOT symmetric menu-card layouts.

HAND-DRAWN ACCENT (Blue Mezcal — OPTIONAL, not every post): Zero or one hand-drawn mark per layout — default is ZERO, add only if the composition asks for it. When used, draw from a small library of BM-world vector ornaments (inked hairline, made-by-human feel): agave leaf or whole plant silhouette, salt-rim arc, citrus wedge cross-section, smoke curl, single dot, mezcal worm outline, hand-drawn hairline underline, ice-cube cube, cocktail-stirrer line. Rotate — agave is ONE option, not mandatory. Never stack multiple ornaments. If the layout already has photo + type doing the work, skip the ornament entirely.

COPY PERSONALITY RULE (critical — NOT every post leads with the dish/drink name):
When option (b) or (c) is used, the subject name can drop to small-caps subhead size or disappear entirely — the photo carries identification. Default: mix across a variation set.

VOICE SEED (Blue Mezcal-only — do not reuse across other brands): editorial-modernist, ENGLISH ONLY (no Spanish — that's Azteca territory), mezcal-reverent, bartender-confident, social-bold. Voice examples: "Smoke, citrus, slow." · "Cool and dangerous." · "One more. You'll survive." · "Impossible to have one." · "Another round?" · "A handshake in a glass." Voice is literary + confident + social, NOT smart-mouth punchy (that's JH) and NOT celebration-warm (that's AZ). Poetic mood-lines, agave-culture references, bartender-confident statements — ALL IN ENGLISH. Never render Spanish phrases on BM posts. When writing COPY OPTION (c), ANALYZE THE SPECIFIC DRINK (spirit base, technique, garnish, feeling on the palate) and write 2–3 candidate lines drawn from THIS BM voice and THIS drink.

TEXTURE: fine noise grain 3–5% on every flat field, subtle handmade paper grain, Portra 400 film grain on photography (daylight-bright, not dark-nocturnal). NO neon, NO velvet burgundy, NO brass foil (that's the nocturnal register).

Photography style: bright editorial daylight 5200K OR soft tungsten 3000K — the drink/dish glows, amber bokeh in the background is fine, but the overall mood is WARM AND INVITING, not dark-moody. 45–60% fill band — editorial restraint with energy.

FORMAT: Editorial hero dish spotlight. Single plate, asymmetric crop, restrained type. One focal point on a rule-of-thirds intersection. Canvas FILL targets (enforce the upper band — no under-filling): Blue Mezcal 50–65% fill, Jackson House 65–75% fill, Azteca 80–95% fill.

COMPOSITION: rule-of-thirds focal point, asymmetric composition, 4:5 portrait; photo fills the canvas at full scale; headline and sub in a color-block field tucked into one corner or band (never shrinking the photo)

HEADLINE OPTIONS (pick ONE — variation-dependent — see COPY PERSONALITY RULE in stem):
  (a) DEFAULT — subject name verbatim, no paraphrasing, no misspellings: "Guacamole con Chicharrón"
  (b) a feeling/mood line derived FROM THIS SPECIFIC DISH (use the body-copy phrase below as raw material — e.g. if the phrase is "Black salt rim. Cucumber crown. Cool and dangerous." a valid mood line is "Cool and dangerous.").
  (c) a personality line DERIVED FROM the specific dish + the brand's voice signature (see VOICE SEED in the brand stem). Do NOT reuse example lines from OTHER brands' voice seeds — generate fresh copy that ONLY makes sense for THIS dish at THIS restaurant.
  Default heavily to (a). Use (b) or (c) only when the line adds real personality and still identifies the dish. If using (b)/(c), subject name may appear as small subhead or be omitted.
BODY COPY (quote exactly when rendered): "Mashed to order."
BOTTOM-MARGIN LINE — render this EXACT text at the bottom margin as a single low-key hairline-ruled line: "826 Kohl Ave · Middletown, DE — Visit us · link in bio"
NO-LITERAL-LABEL RULE (non-negotiable): NEVER print any of these meta-label words on the final image — they are instructions to you, NOT content to render: CTA, ADDRESS, SUPPORT LINE, FOOTER LINE, BOTTOM-MARGIN LINE, HEADLINE, SUBHEAD, BODY COPY, HEADER, LABEL, COPY OPTION, VARIATION, WORDMARK, AWARD BADGE, WORD-SPILL, DEFAULT. If any of those words leak onto the canvas, the image fails review.
LOGO IDENTITY LOCK (NON-NEGOTIABLE — HIGHEST PRIORITY):
  A second reference image is attached — the brand's REAL, APPROVED logo.
  You MUST composite the supplied logo PNG VERBATIM at 80–120px tall (~7–10% of
  canvas width) in ONE corner: bottom-center, bottom-left, bottom-right, or top-right.

  YOU MUST NOT:
    - redraw, re-letter, re-illustrate, restyle, or recolour the logo
    - invent an alternate version of the warrior / wordmark / brand mark
    - combine the logo with other elements inside the logo itself
    - generate a 'stylized' or 'custom' version of the logo
    - render the brand name as display type anywhere outside the composited logo
    - render a faux logo badge, crest, seal, or lockup containing the brand name

  The brand name may appear ONLY inside the composited real-logo PNG. All other
  typography on the canvas is headline, subhead, or support copy — never a recreation
  of the brand logo. Any output that redraws the logo is REJECTED.

VARIATION DIRECTION: PAINTED BRUSH POSTER. Photo cut out and painted over with gouache brush-strokes in cobalt+cream — the drink becomes a painted gesture not a photo. One italic serif line. Gallery-print register.

PHOTO RULE: HERO-PHOTO RULE (HIGHEST PRIORITY — cannot be violated):
  1. The reference food photograph supplied as input is the CANVAS FOUNDATION of this      design. It fills the primary region of the composition at FULL SCALE. It is NEVER      reduced to a small swatch, tile, inset, thumbnail, or decorative element. The photo      is the hero; the template is LAID OVER IT.
  2. Typography, colour fields, motifs, and ornament sit OVER or ADJACENT TO the full-     size photo — they do not shrink the photo to make room. If a headline needs space,      use an upper-third or lower-third color-block field ON TOP of the photo, or extend      negative brand-color margin from one edge of the photo outward (photo still fills      60–85% of canvas minimum).
  3. DO NOT regenerate, restyle, or alter the plating, lighting, colours, or contents      of the food in any way. The photo must remain photographically identical to the source.
  4. Variations that shrink, miniaturize, inset, or tile the photo are REJECTED.      (User feedback, BM-A1-002: "I don't want smaller images of what we have, use      the real images as the template, and design OVER them.")

PHOTO-LED PALETTE COMPLEMENT RULE:
  The brand-palette colours you choose for headline fields, accent blocks, and type   MUST COMPLEMENT the photo's dominant hue, not clash with it. If the photo is warm-  dominant (amber, orange, red, bourbon), pull cooler brand colours (cobalt, deep   navy, forest) for contrast. If the photo is cool-dominant (blue, green, grey), pull   warmer brand colours (brass, oxblood, marigold). Name the photo's dominant hue in   your head first, then pick the brand-palette hex that complements it.

CONTRAST GUARD (non-negotiable, applies to EVERY text-over-anything decision):
  Before finalizing the layout, estimate the luminance of the type and the luminance of
  whatever sits behind it (ground field OR the pixels of the photo under the type).
  If both are dark (type luma <30%% AND ground luma <40%%) OR both are light (type luma
  >70%% AND ground luma >60%%), REJECT and re-choose. Type must read at arm's length on
  a phone — if legibility is marginal, change the type hex to the opposite luminance pole
  of the brand palette. No dark-on-dark, no light-on-light, ever.

TEXT-FIT-CONTAINER RULE (non-negotiable):
  Type SIZES TO THE CONTAINER, not the other way around. If the headline doesn't fit at
  a legible size in the designated color-block / strip / wedge, choose ONE of: (i) shrink
  the type to legibility-min only IF it still reads at arm's length, (ii) expand the
  container to match the type, (iii) break the headline across 2–3 lines with generous
  leading. NEVER cram letters, NEVER rotate 90° to fit a narrow strip, NEVER letter-clip
  at the canvas edge. If a chosen variation angle forces any of these, pick a better
  container geometry.

NO-HERO-OCCLUSION RULE:
  Diagonal wedges, horizontal bands across the middle third, and any geometric shape that
  CUTS THROUGH the primary photo region are forbidden. Color fields sit adjacent to the
  photo, extend from one edge inward, or occupy a corner — they never bisect the dish or
  glass. If the chosen variation angle creates a slash through the hero, reshape it.

NEGATIVE-CONTENT LIST (NON-NEGOTIABLE — REJECT IF ANY APPEAR ON CANVAS):
  Never render these words, phrases, or visual references anywhere on the image,
  regardless of brand or content type:
    - 'La Bamba' / 'La Bamba Ice Cream' — different brand, never on El Azteca posts
    - 'Cumbia Night' / 'live music' — unverified events, do not invent
    - 'Altar Menu' / 'Ofrenda Menu' / 'Menu Especial' — unverified seasonal menus
    - 'Five Course' / 'Prix Fixe' / '28-day Residency' — do not invent event details
    - 'Blue Mezcal Quarterly' / any fake magazine or publication title
    - 'Chef's Tasting' / 'Chef's Table' unless supplied as real support copy
    - Any named staff ('Selena', 'Marco', 'Elena', 'the House') on any brand other
      than the one where that person actually works
    - Any phone number, website URL, social handle, or price not supplied
  If a layout feels empty without such copy, REDUCE copy rather than invent.

FACTUAL-INTEGRITY RULE (NON-NEGOTIABLE — HIGHEST PRIORITY):
  This is real restaurant marketing for real clients. Every word rendered on the image
  MUST be factually accurate. DO NOT INVENT:
    - Staff names, titles, or roles (a person named on a post must actually work there)
    - Magazine / publication / quarterly titles ('Blue Mezcal Quarterly' does not exist)
    - Issue numbers, edition labels, 'Vol. II' references
    - Event names not supplied in this prompt
    - Drink names, dish names, prices, dates, times not supplied
    - Award names, certifications, rankings
    - 'Est. [year]' dates unless supplied
    - Fake product lines, merchandise, collaborations
  ONLY use factual content that comes from these exact sources:
    1. The 'subject' field supplied in this prompt
    2. The 'support' field supplied in this prompt
    3. The brand profile's documented address, wordmark, and operational lines
    4. Documented voice seeds (dichos, signatures) written in the brand stem
  If a layout feels empty without more copy, REDUCE THE COPY rather than invent — negative
  space is always better than fabricated facts.

CROSS-BRAND ISOLATION (non-negotiable):
  Staff, bartender, and chef names are brand-specific and NEVER cross between brands.
  - Selena = Jackson House bartender ONLY. Never render 'Selena' on a Blue Mezcal or
    Azteca post under any circumstance.
  - Do not invent team-member names for any brand. If no real staff name is supplied,
    skip the personality-credit line entirely.

REAL-IMAGE IDENTITY LOCK (NON-NEGOTIABLE — HIGHEST PRIORITY):
  The attached reference photograph of the dish/drink is REAL PROFESSIONAL PHOTOGRAPHY
  produced by Savora. It is NOT a style reference, NOT a prompt, NOT inspiration. It is
  the FINAL DISH IMAGE that must appear verbatim in the output.

  YOU MUST NOT:
    - regenerate, redraw, repaint, or restyle the dish
    - substitute a different-looking version of the dish
    - change the angle, lighting, plating, garnish, sauce pattern, char marks, glass shape,
      ice, crema, herbs, or any visible element of the actual food
    - apply a filter, stylization, or 'photographic re-interpretation' to the plate
    - generate a new photograph that looks similar but is not the reference
    - AI-hallucinate plausible food textures in place of the real pixels

  YOU MUST:
    - treat the reference photograph as a LOCKED COMPOSITE LAYER — pixel-accurate, placed
      into the design untouched
    - match the reference frame-for-frame (same crop, same color, same shadows, same
      moisture on the glass, same grill marks on the steak, same garnish position)
    - compose the graphic design AROUND, BESIDE, or ON TOP OF the locked photo — never
      regenerate the photo itself
    - if the layout needs the photo cropped, silhouetted, or cut-out, do so using the
      ACTUAL reference pixels — not a re-painted imitation

  This is a Savora brand rule. Serving AI-generated food imagery as our photography is
  a breach of client trust. The dish pixels are sacred. Design around them.

LUXURY-TEXTURE RULE (non-negotiable):
  * Every colour field MUST have a subtle tonal gradient (5–12% shift from one edge to     the opposite edge). NEVER use a pure flat hex fill. Flat fills read cheap and digital.
  * Every colour field MUST carry a texture overlay: 3–8% noise/grain is the minimum.     Add one of: cream laid paper, linen weave, letterpress emboss, brass foil, film     grain, halftone dots, or risograph misregistration — whichever matches the register.
  * Every typographic element MUST carry texture too: letterpress emboss for Jackson     House (type sits AS IF pressed into cream paper, 2px inner-shadow dark edge + 1px     light highlight); brass foil with subtle specular for Blue Mezcal nocturnal; ink-    bleed / halftone offset for Azteca playful; paper-grain impression for Blue Mezcal     editorial.
  * Photography receives Portra 400 or Portra 800 film-grain emulation (depending on     lighting register) — never a clean digital file.
  * Where a drop shadow is used, follow the pro spec: y-offset 4–8px, blur 16–40px,     opacity 8–20%, colour = a DARKER SHADE of the background (never pure black).     NEVER the default Photoshop 5px-90°-75%-black drop shadow.

LUXURY-GRADIENT EXAMPLES (pick one that fits):
  * Paper-gradient: cream lightens toward the centre, darkens at the edges (letterpress     vignette).
  * Tonal-brand-gradient: cobalt #1E3A8A → #1A3275 in a 5% shift across the field.
  * Foil-gradient: brass #C9A24B → #B08937 → #C9A24B as a soft 3-stop reading like a     foil-stamp highlight catching light.
  * Velvet-gradient: burgundy #4A1621 falling into charred black #0B0B0B at one corner,     like low candlelight.

DROP-SHADOW RULE:
  Only add a drop shadow to type when the palette contrast is INSUFFICIENT on its own   (e.g., cream type over a busy mid-tone photo). When contrast is already strong   (e.g., brass-foil gold on deep forest green, cream on charred black), OMIT drop   shadows entirely — they cheapen the foil-stamp effect. Foil type on a deep tonal   field needs no shadow; the foil itself carries the hierarchy.

FINAL: Render at the aspect ratio specified via the API (not in prose). Dense typography with purpose. Real brand register. No stock gradients, no Canva-template feel. Avoid the AI tells: no cyan-to-magenta gradients, no default Poppins/Montserrat/Open Sans, no 33/33/33 colour balance, no centered dead-symmetric layouts unless ceremonial, no flat-colour fields. Every surface is LAYERED: photo + gradient + noise + emboss / foil / grain.
